steps.title: Identify the shared view
steps.content: Drake values Parisi's focus on the false starts and mistakes of real scientific research. She argues that scientists should include this “human stuff” to show how science actually gets done.

steps.title: Reject unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A invents a difference between Drake and Parisi. Choice C focuses on nonfiction narrative as a way to share accomplishments, which is not the main point. Choice D invents a claim about Parisi's modesty.

steps.title: State the passage purpose
steps.content: The remaining choice captures the main purpose: the text outlines an area of agreement between Parisi and Drake about showing the imperfect, human process of science. The answer is B.
